title: Legazpia mucronulata
intro: Legazpia mucronulata, also known as Bagtikan, is a plant species native to the Philippines, belonging to the family Annonaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help in treating various conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Legazpia mucronulata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Filipino medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various ailments.
